perturbation.original_text: THIS MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT ("Agreement"), dated as of 09/24/2018 (the "Effective Date"), is between Clear Capital (the "Company") and RadialSpark, LLC (the "Contractor"). WHEREAS, Company desires from time to time to retain Contractor to perform certain management consulting services for Company; and WHEREAS, Contractor desires to perform such management consulting services for Company;
perturbation.changed_text: THIS MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT ("Agreement"), dated as of 09/24/2018 (the "Effective Date"), is between Clear Capital (the "Company") and RadialSpark, LLC (the "Consultant"). WHEREAS, Company desires from time to time to retain Consultant to perform certain management consulting services for Company; and WHEREAS, Consultant desires to perform such management consulting services for Company;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Contractor' to 'Consultant' in the introductory paragraph creates confusion, as the agreement consistently refers to RadialSpark, LLC as 'Contractor' throughout the rest of the document. This could lead to disputes about whether provisions applying to the 'Contractor' also apply to RadialSpark, LLC, since it is also referred to as 'Consultant' here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the foregoing premises, and the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, the parties hereto agree as follows: 1. PURPOSE OF ENGAGEMENT. (a) Company agrees to retain Contractor to perform the consulting services for Company, on a task by task basis (the "Services"), and Contractor agrees to furnish the Services on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'Contractor' with 'Consultant' in the introductory paragraph, while the agreement consistently refers to RadialSpark, LLC as 'Contractor' in other sections, specifically clause 1(a). This creates a direct contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about which provisions apply to RadialSpark, LLC, potentially leading to disputes and making the contract unenforceable due to the misaligned terminology. The contradiction is strong as clause 1(a) explicitly states 'Company agrees to retain Contractor', whereas the changed text introduces 'Consultant' without clarification. This breaks the compatibility between the introductory definition and the operational clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor shall provide sufficient qualified personnel to perform the Services in a professional and workmanlike manner in accordance with industry standards. A Statement of Work may designate certain individuals as "Key Personnel" for an engagement, and the parties subsequently may agree in writing that additional individuals are Key Personnel for such engagement. If there are Key Personnel for an engagement, Contractor shall provide the Services through those personnel and such additional personnel of Contractor as Contractor may from time to time determine to be required for the performance of the Services. Company shall have the right to interview and approve such additional personnel at Company's request. If one or more Key Personnel terminate their employment with Contractor or otherwise become Page 2 of 10 unavailable to work on an engagement for reasons beyond Contractor's reasonable control, Contractor may provide the Services through other personnel with comparable training and experience. If Company becomes dissatisfied with any of Contractor's personnel providing the Services, Company may notify Contractor of the details of its dissatisfaction, and the parties shall cooperate to remedy the problem as soon as possible. If Company reasonably requests, Contractor shall reassign the individual who is the subject of Company's dissatisfaction and replace that person with other personnel in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The consulting company shall provide sufficient qualified personnel to perform the Services in a professional and workmanlike manner in accordance with industry standards. A Statement of Work may designate certain individuals as "Key Personnel" for an engagement, and the parties subsequently may agree in writing that additional individuals are Key Personnel for such engagement. If there are Key Personnel for an engagement, the consulting company shall provide the Services through those personnel and such additional personnel of the consulting company as the consulting company may from time to time determine to be required for the performance of the Services. Company shall have the right to interview and approve such additional personnel at Company's request. If one or more Key Personnel terminate their employment with the consulting company or otherwise become Page 2 of 10 unavailable to work on an engagement for reasons beyond the consulting company's reasonable control, the consulting company may provide the Services through other personnel with comparable training and experience. If Company becomes dissatisfied with any of the consulting company's personnel providing the Services, Company may notify the consulting company of the details of its dissatisfaction, and the parties shall cooperate to remedy the problem as soon as possible. If Company reasonably requests, the consulting company shall reassign the individual who is the subject of Company's dissatisfaction and replace that person with other personnel in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Contractor' to 'the consulting company' creates confusion about which entity these clauses refer to since it is not the name being used on the agreement. This creates an internal contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement. It is not clear that 'the consulting company' is reffering to the radialspark, LLC
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) To the extent Contractor provides any third party materials and products Contractor acknowledges that it shall be solely responsible for ensuring the functionality and specifications of such third party materials and products used in performing Services under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Contractor' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Contractor' with 'the consulting company.' This creates ambiguity because it's not explicitly stated that 'the consulting company' refers to the Contractor, Radialspark, LLC. The Contradicted Text references Contractor, and if the Changed Text is referencing a different party, then the changed text directly creates a conflicting obligation. Specifically, the liability for third-party materials and products shifts from the clearly defined 'Contractor' to the ambiguous 'consulting company,' creating a significant conflict. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date hereof and shall continue until terminated by either party pursuant to Paragraph 6 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date hereof and shall continue until cancelled by either party pursuant to Paragraph 6 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'terminated' with 'cancelled,' we create a scenario where the terms for ending the agreement are unclear. While 'terminated' is legally precise and linked to Paragraph 6, 'cancelled' is vaguer. This can create ambiguity. The section 6 is still reffering to 'terminated' so it creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION. (a) At any time that there is no uncompleted Statement of Work outstanding, either party may terminate this Agreement for any or no reason upon fifteen (15) days advance notice to the other. (b) In addition, either party may terminate this Agreement or any outstanding Statement of Work, upon fifteen (15) days written notice to the other party, in the event such other party breaches a material term of this Agreement or any Statement of Work and such breach remains uncured at the end of such fifteen (15) day period. Upon any such termination, Contractor will be paid all fees and expenses that have been incurred or earned in connection with the performance of the Services through the effective date of such termination. Contractor shall provide to Company, and hereby assigns to Company, all right, title and interest to any Works in progress.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'terminated' to 'cancelled' introduces ambiguity regarding the proper method for ending the agreement, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. Paragraph 6, the 'Contradicted Text,' explicitly uses the term 'terminate' and outlines specific conditions for termination. The change causes the document to use different terms to specify the same action (ending the agreement) which creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as defined above.
